imagePullSecrets.description: Global Docker registry secret names as an array

defaultStorageClass.description: Global default StorageClass for Persistent Volume(s)

adaptSecurityContext.description: Adapt the securityContext sections of the deployment to make them compatible with Openshift restricted-v2 SCC: remove runAsUser, runAsGroup and fsGroup and let the platform use their allowed default IDs. Possible values: auto (apply if the detected running cluster is Openshift), force (perform the adaptation always), disabled (do not perform adaptation)

global.description:
Copyright Broadcom, Inc. All Rights Reserved.
SPDX-License-Identifier: APACHE-2.0
Global Docker image parameters
Please, note that this will override the image parameters, including dependencies, configured to use the global value
Current available global Docker image parameters: imageRegistry, imagePullSecrets and storageClass

kubeVersion.description: Force target Kubernetes version (using Helm capabilities if not set)

extraDeploy.description: Array of extra objects to deploy with the release

nameOverride.description: String to partially override grafana.fullname template (will maintain the release name)

fullnameOverride.description: String to fully override grafana.fullname template

commonLabels.description: Labels to add to all deployed objects

commonAnnotations.description: Annotations to add to all deployed objects

usePasswordFiles.description: Mount credentials as files instead of using environment variables

digest.description: Grafana image digest in the way sha256:aa.... Please note this parameter, if set, will override the tag

password.description: Admin password. If a password is not provided a random password will be generated

existingSecret.description: Name of the existing secret containing admin password

existingSecretPasswordKey.description: Password key on the existing secret

host.description: Custom host for the smtp server

existingSecret.description: Name of existing secret containing SMTP credentials (user and password)

existingSecretUserKey.description: User key on the existing secret

existingSecretPasswordKey.description: Password key on the existing secret

plugins.description: Grafana plugins to be installed in deployment time separated by commas

allowSignUp.description: Allows LDAP sign up for Grafana

configuration.description: Specify content for ldap.toml configuration file

configMapName.description: Name of the ConfigMap with the ldap.toml configuration file for Grafana

secretName.description: Name of the Secret with the ldap.toml configuration file for Grafana

binddn.description: DN of the account used to search in the LDAP server.

basedn.description: Base DN path where binddn account will search for the users.

searchAttribute.description: Field used to match with the user name (uid, samAccountName, cn, etc). This value will be ignored if 'ldap.searchFilter' is set

searchFilter.description: User search filter, for example "(cn=%s)" or "(sAMAccountName=%s)" or "(|(sAMAccountName=%s)(userPrincipalName=%s)"

skipVerify.description: Skip any SSL verification (hostanames or certificates)

CAFilename.description: CA certificate filename. Should match with the CA entry key in the ldap.tls.certificatesSecret.

certFilename.description: Client certificate filename to authenticate against the LDAP server. Should match with certificate the entry key in the ldap.tls.certificatesSecret.

certKeyFilename.description: Client Key filename to authenticate against the LDAP server. Should match with certificate the entry key in the ldap.tls.certificatesSecret.

enabled.description: Enable using a remote rendering service to render PNG images

serverURL.description: URL of the remote rendering service

imageRenderer.description: Grafana Image Renderer configuration for Grafana

grafanaIniConfigMap.description: Name of the ConfigMap containing the `grafana.ini` file

grafanaIniSecret.description: Name of the Secret containing the `grafana.ini` file

config.description:
Parameters to override the default grafana.ini file.
It is needed to create a configmap or a secret containing the grafana.ini file.

enabled.description: Enable the use of a Grafana dashboard provider

configMapName.description: Name of a ConfigMap containing a custom dashboard provider

dashboardsProvider.description:
Create dasboard provider to load dashboards, a default one is created to load dashboards
from "/opt/bitnami/grafana/dashboards"

dashboardsConfigMaps.description: Array with the names of a series of ConfigMaps containing dashboards files

secretName.description: The name of an externally-managed secret containing custom datasource files.

secretDefinition.description: The contents of a secret defining a custom datasource file. Only used if datasources.secretName is empty or not defined.

datasources.description: Import datasources from an externally-managed secret, or a secret definition set via Helm values.

configMapName.description: Name of a ConfigMap containing Grafana notifiers configuration

notifiers.description:
Create notifiers from a configMap
The notifiersName must contain the files

configMapName.description: Name of a ConfigMap containing Grafana alerting configuration

alerting.description: Create alerting rules, contact points, notification policies, templates, and mute timings from a configMap

type.description: Set up update strategy for Grafana installation.

updateStrategy.description:
Set to Recreate if you use persistent volume that cannot be mounted by more than one pods to make sure the pods is destroyed first.
ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/#strategy
Example:
updateStrategy:
 type: RollingUpdate
 rollingUpdate:
   maxSurge: 25%
   maxUnavailable: 25%

automountServiceAccountToken.description: Mount Service Account token in pod

terminationGracePeriodSeconds.description: In seconds, time the given to the Grafana pod needs to terminate gracefully

podAffinityPreset.description: Pod affinity preset. Ignored if `affinity` is set. Allowed values: `soft` or `hard`

podAntiAffinityPreset.description: Pod anti-affinity preset. Ignored if `affinity` is set. Allowed values: `soft` or `hard`

type.description: Node affinity preset type. Ignored if `affinity` is set. Allowed values: `soft` or `hard`

key.description: Node label key to match Ignored if `affinity` is set.

values.description: Node label values to match. Ignored if `affinity` is set.

topologySpreadConstraints.description: Topology spread constraints rely on node labels to identify the topology domain(s) that each Node is in

enabled.description: Enable securityContext on for Grafana deployment

sysctls.description: Set kernel settings using the sysctl interface

fsGroup.description: Group to configure permissions for volumes

drop.description: List of capabilities to be dropped

type.description: Set container's Security Context seccomp profile

resourcesPreset.description: Set container resources according to one common preset (allowed values: none, nano, micro, small, medium, large, xlarge, 2xlarge). This is ignored if grafana.resources is set (grafana.resources is recommended for production).

resources.description: Set container requests and limits for different resources like CPU or memory (essential for production workloads)

customLivenessProbe.description: Custom livenessProbe that overrides the default one

customReadinessProbe.description: Custom readinessProbe that overrides the default one

customStartupProbe.description: Custom startupProbe that overrides the default one

lifecycleHooks.description: for the Grafana container(s) to automate configuration before or after startup

sidecars.description: Attach additional sidecar containers to the Grafana pod

initContainers.description: Add additional init containers to the Grafana pod(s)

enableServiceLinks.description: Whether information about services should be injected into pod's environment variable

extraVolumes.description: Additional volumes for the Grafana pod

extraVolumeMounts.description: Additional volume mounts for the Grafana container

extraEnvVarsCM.description: Name of existing ConfigMap containing extra env vars for Grafana nodes

extraEnvVarsCMOptional.description: Whether to still run the Grafana node if the ConfigMap does not exist

extraEnvVarsSecret.description: Name of existing Secret containing extra env vars for Grafana nodes

extraEnvVarsSecretOptional.description: Whether to still run the Grafana node if the Secret does not exist

extraEnvVars.description: Array containing extra env vars to configure Grafana

extraConfigmaps.description: Array to mount extra ConfigMaps to configure Grafana

command.description: Override default container command (useful when using custom images)

args.description: Override default container args (useful when using custom images)

create.description: Enable/disable a Pod Disruption Budget creation

minAvailable.description: Minimum number/percentage of pods that should remain scheduled

maxUnavailable.description: Maximum number/percentage of pods that may be made unavailable. Defaults to `1` if both `grafana.pdb.minAvailable` and `grafana.pdb.maxUnavailable` are empty.

storageClass.description: Storage class to use with the PVC

existingClaim.description: If you want to reuse an existing claim, you can pass the name of the PVC using the existingClaim variable. Please note that this setting will be ignored when `grafana.kind` is set to `StatefulSet`.

persistence.description:
Enable persistence using Persistent Volume Claims
ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes/

create.description: Specifies whether a ServiceAccount should be created

name.description: The name of the ServiceAccount to use. If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the ServiceAccount Metadata

automountServiceAccountToken.description: Automount service account token for the application controller service account

grafana.description: Specify the nodePort value for the LoadBalancer and NodePort service types

loadBalancerIP.description: loadBalancerIP if Grafana service type is `LoadBalancer` (optional, cloud specific)

loadBalancerClass.description: loadBalancerClass if Grafana service type is `LoadBalancer` (optional, cloud specific)

loadBalancerSourceRanges.description: loadBalancerSourceRanges if Grafana service type is `LoadBalancer` (optional, cloud specific)

annotations.description: Provide any additional annotations which may be required.

extraPorts.description: Extra port to expose on Grafana service

sessionAffinity.description: Session Affinity for Kubernetes service, can be "None" or "ClientIP"

enabled.description: Specifies whether a NetworkPolicy should be created

allowExternal.description: Don't require server label for connections

allowExternalEgress.description: Allow the pod to access any range of port and all destinations.

addExternalClientAccess.description: Allow access from pods with client label set to "true". Ignored if `networkPolicy.allowExternal` is true.

extraIngress.description: Add extra ingress rules to the NetworkPolicy

extraEgress.description: Add extra ingress rules to the NetworkPolicy

ingressPodMatchLabels.description: Labels to match to allow traffic from other pods. Ignored if `networkPolicy.allowExternal` is true.

ingressNSMatchLabels.description: Labels to match to allow traffic from other namespaces. Ignored if `networkPolicy.allowExternal` is true.

ingressNSPodMatchLabels.description: Pod labels to match to allow traffic from other namespaces. Ignored if `networkPolicy.allowExternal` is true.

enabled.description: Set to true to enable ingress record generation

apiVersion.description: Override API Version (automatically detected if not set)

hostname.description: When the ingress is enabled, a host pointing to this will be created

path.description: Default path for the ingress resource

annotations.description: Additional annotations for the Ingress resource. To enable certificate autogeneration, place here your cert-manager annotations.

tls.description: Enable TLS configuration for the hostname defined at ingress.hostname parameter

extraHosts.description: The list of additional hostnames to be covered with this ingress record.

extraPaths.description: Any additional arbitrary paths that may need to be added to the ingress under the main host.

extraTls.description: The tls configuration for additional hostnames to be covered with this ingress record.

secrets.description: It is also possible to create and manage the certificates outside of this helm chart

selfSigned.description: Create a TLS secret for this ingress record using self-signed certificates generated by Helm

ingressClassName.description: IngressClass that will be be used to implement the Ingress (Kubernetes 1.18+)

extraRules.description: Additional rules to be covered with this ingress record

ingress.description:
Configure the ingress resource that allows you to access the
Grafana installation. Set up the URL
ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/ingress/

enabled.description: Enable the export of Prometheus metrics
if `true`, creates a Prometheus Operator ServiceMonitor (also requires `metrics.enabled` to be `true`)

namespace.description: Namespace in which Prometheus is running

interval.description: Interval at which metrics should be scraped.

scrapeTimeout.description: Timeout after which the scrape is ended

relabelings.description: RelabelConfigs to apply to samples before scraping

metricRelabelings.description: MetricRelabelConfigs to apply to samples before ingestion

honorLabels.description: Labels to honor to add to the scrape endpoint

labels.description: Additional custom labels for the ServiceMonitor

jobLabel.description: The name of the label on the target service to use as the job name in prometheus.

enabled.description: if `true`, creates a Prometheus Operator PrometheusRule (also requires `metrics.enabled` to be `true` and `metrics.prometheusRule.rules`)

namespace.description: Namespace for the PrometheusRule Resource (defaults to the Release Namespace)

additionalLabels.description: Additional labels that can be used so PrometheusRule will be discovered by Prometheus

enabled.description: Enable init container that changes the owner/group of the PV mount point to `runAsUser:fsGroup`

digest.description: OS Shell + Utility image digest in the way sha256:aa.... Please note this parameter, if set, will override the tag

pullPolicy.description: OS Shell + Utility image pull policy

pullSecrets.description: OS Shell + Utility image pull secrets

resourcesPreset.description: Set container resources according to one common preset (allowed values: none, nano, micro, small, medium, large, xlarge, 2xlarge). This is ignored if volumePermissions.resources is set (volumePermissions.resources is recommended for production).

resources.description: Set container requests and limits for different resources like CPU or memory (essential for production workloads)

runAsUser.description: Set init container's Security Context runAsUser

containerSecurityContext.description:
Init container Container Security Context
ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container
NOTE: when runAsUser is set to special value "auto", init container will try to chown the
  data folder to auto-determined user&group, using commands: `id -u`:`id -G | cut -d" " -f2`
  "auto" is especially useful for OpenShift which has scc with dynamic user ids (and 0 is not allowed)

volumePermissions.description:
'volumePermissions' init container parameters
Changes the owner and group of the persistent volume mount point to runAsUser:fsGroup values
  based on the `grafana:podSecurityContext`/`grafana:containerSecurityContext`` parameters
May require setting `grafana:podSecurityContext:runAsNonRoot` to false

enabled.description: Enable diagnostic mode (all probes will be disabled and the command will be overridden)

command.description: Command to override all containers in the deployment

args.description: Args to override all containers in the deployment

diagnosticMode.description: Enable diagnostic mode in the deployment
